body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready
applicable: yes

## Reasoning

`self-correction-iteration` (scenario-meanings.md): the defining behavior is an agent that runs a tool, sees the result FAIL, interprets the failure, and retries with corrected input â€” multiple tool-call/result cycles WITHIN one turn â€” with the session held continuously in `working` and NO flicker to `ready` or `waiting` between attempts. The **Primitive exercised is `tool_calls` + iterative retry within a single turn**; the load-bearing irrlicht primitive is the classifier holding `working` across multi-step retry chains that span multiple tool-call/result pairs without an intervening turn boundary. Explicitly *Not to be confused with* `long-agentic-session-stress` (many separate user-driven turns) or `turn-aborted-by-error` (agent gives up and the provider error closes the turn).

**agent_supports: yes.** Codex CLI 0.130.0 drives shell work through its exec/`function_call` tool and iterates autonomously within a single turn: when a command exits non-zero it reads the error and issues a corrective command before the turn ends. A prompt of the form "run `cat /nonexistent`; if it fails, instead run `ls` and report" deterministically produces a failing `function_call` immediately followed by a corrective `function_call` in the same turn, exactly the spec's retry-within-one-turn shape. `capabilities.json` records `tool_calls: true`.

**daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready.** The codex parser (parser.go) models tool calls as paired `function_call` (open) / `function_call_output` (close) events. A FAILING command still emits a `function_call_output` (carrying the non-zero/error result) which CLOSES the open call â€” so `HasOpenToolCall` clears and the classifier never treats a tool failure as a `waiting` gate. The turn settles to `ready` only at the canonical `task_complete` â†’ `turn_done` signal (parser.go lines 155-170), which the codex parser maps explicitly to suppress the assistant_message workingâ†’readyâ†’working flicker. Net observable: one `ready â†’ working` at the user prompt, a single contiguous `working` span across the errored function_call_output AND the corrective function_call/output, then one `working â†’ ready` at task_complete. This is precisely the spec's three Expected bullets, and the no-mid-turn-flip invariant is exactly what the codex turn_done/open-call logic guarantees.

This cell is observationally DISTINCT from the already-covered `codex Ă— auto-executed-tool-call`: that cell captures a single happy-path tool call (readyâ†’workingâ†’ready, one open/close), whereas this cell asserts the classifier's continuity across an ERRORED tool result followed by a recovery tool within the same working span â€” the multi-cycle no-flicker invariant the happy path can't exercise.

## Conclusion

Applicable: yes. codex naturally self-corrects within one turn, and the daemon observes the full arc as a single contiguous `working` span (errored function_call_output never flips to waiting/ready; task_complete settles to ready). Recipe: an interactive prompt that induces a failing-then-corrected exec sequence in one turn (e.g. `cat` a nonexistent file then fall back to `ls`), wait_turn, trailing sleep. Spec asserts user-observable state/topology only (readyâ†’working, intermediate no-flicker working span, workingâ†’ready) â€” no tool error text.
caveats: Assert user-observable state/topology only â€” do NOT assert the specific exec tool error text, which is model/OS-dependent and not stable across re-records.

Distinct from the committed `codex Ă— auto-executed-tool-call` cell: that captures a single happy-path open/close tool call; this cell exercises the no-flicker continuity across an errored result plus a recovery tool within the same working span.

sources.note: self-correction-iteration: agent fails a tool call, retries with corrected input within one turn; state stays working across retries with no flicker; primitive = tool_calls + iterative retry; distinct from long-agentic-session-stress and turn-aborted-by-error
Feature: Self-correction iteration â€” Expected: readyâ†’working; state stays working across all retry cycles (no flicker to ready/waiting); workingâ†’ready after final attempt
tool_calls: true â€” codex iterates tool calls autonomously within a turn

function_call/function_call_output modeled as paired open/close; a failing command still emits function_call_output which clears HasOpenToolCall (no waiting); task_completeâ†’turn_done is the only ready settle, suppressing mid-turn flicker
neighbouring cell captures the single happy-path tool call; this cell adds the errored-then-recovered multi-cycle continuity the happy path can't exercise
